bio: Edmund Henry Stuenkel Jr. was part of the Palmer, Washington County, Kansas Stuenkel family and later died in Fremont, Dodge County, Nebraska. A Find A Grave memorial matching his 1918-1976 dates preserves his burial record and confirms the Kansas/Nebraska family footprint.
records: Find A Grave memorial #42870488 for Edmund Henry Stuenkel Jr.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Edmund Henry Stuenkel Jr.

bio: Edmund Henry Stuenkel Sr. was born in Sterling, Nebraska, and spent his later life in Washington County, Kansas. His Find A Grave memorial places his burial in Washington Cemetery, Washington County, Kansas, tying this branch to the north-central Kansas Lutheran/German-settler community.
records: Find A Grave memorial #98758297 for Edmund Henry Stuenkel Sr.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Edmund Henry Stuenkel Sr.

bio: Bertha A. Gabbert married Edmund H. Stuenkel and is recorded in the Palmer, Washington County, Kansas area. Her burial is recorded at Saint Johns Lutheran Cemetery in Palmer, reflecting the family’s continued connection to German Lutheran communities in Kansas.
records: Find A Grave memorial #98329539 for Bertha A. Gabbert Stuenkel.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Bertha A. Gabbert Stuenkel

bio: Dietrich Louis August Stuenkel appears in later records as August Louis “Gus” Stuenkel of Linn, Washington County, Kansas. A genealogy summary says he died suddenly at the family home in Linn and was a well-known local figure.
funFacts: He and Christine M. Stuenkel were reported to have at least 6 sons and 3 daughters.
records: Find A Grave memorial #113231341 for August Louis Stuenkel.

citations.label: Find A Grave: August Louis Stuenkel

bio: Maria Dorothea Christine Stuenkel appears in FamilySearch/Find A Grave-indexed material as Christine M. Stuenkel. Those records identify her parents as Friedrich Heinrich Konrad Stuenkel and Sophie Charlotte Frerking, and say she married August L. Stuenkel on 25 July 1878 in Lafayette County, Missouri.
funFacts: The FamilySearch summary reports that Christine and August L. Stuenkel had at least 6 sons and 3 daughters.
records: FamilySearch ancestor profile LR32-964.
Find A Grave memorial #113231470 for Christine M. Stuenkel.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Christine M. Stuenkel

bio: Heinrich Emil “Henry” Gabbert was a German-born settler who lived in Linn, Washington County, Kansas. Search-indexed cemetery and genealogy records identify him as a farmer, husband of Caroline Schmidt, and father of several children including Bertha A. Stuenkel.
funFacts: Find A Grave places him in Zion Lutheran Cemetery at Linn, Kansas; search snippets report a death-date discrepancy of 21 July 1917 versus the roster’s 23 July 1917.
immigration: Born in Prussia/Germany and settled in Washington County, Kansas; no ship or arrival year was confirmed in the sources found.
records: Find A Grave memorial #113812084 for Heinrich Emil “Henry” Gabbert.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Heinrich Emil Gabbert

bio: Caroline Schmidt was the wife of Heinrich Emil “Henry” Gabbert in the German Lutheran community around Linn, Washington County, Kansas. Find A Grave-indexed records place her burial with the Gabbert family at Zion Lutheran Cemetery in Linn.
funFacts: The Gabbert family search result names children including Bertha A. Stuenkel, Emilie O. Timme, Heinrich Henry Peter T. Gabbert, and Emil William Gabbert.
records: Find A Grave memorial #113812127 for Caroline Gabbert.

bio: A search-indexed Find A Grave memorial matches Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel with the 1808-1876 dates in the roster and a Lafayette County, Missouri connection. Because the roster has an apparent duplicate for the same name and dates, this same cautious record note is attached to both IDs.
records: Find A Grave memorial #27728629 for Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel

bio: A search-indexed Find A Grave memorial matches Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel with the 1808-1876 dates in the roster and a Lafayette County, Missouri connection. Because the roster has an apparent duplicate for the same name and dates, this same cautious record note is attached to both IDs.
records: Find A Grave memorial #27728629 for Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel

bio: WikiTree lists Louisa Elizabeth Scharnhorst Stuenkel as a daughter of Louis Scharnhorst and Louise Gasdorff. The same profile records her death in 1869 and burial in Saint Pauls Lutheran Cemetery, Concordia, Lafayette County, Missouri.
records: WikiTree profile Scharnhorst-117.
Find A Grave memorial #35135471, cited by WikiTree.

bio: Sophie Charlotte Frerking appears in her mother Dorothea Sprengel Frerking’s 1850 census discussion as Charlotte Stuenkle, age 27, born in Germany. She was living in District 46, Lafayette County, Missouri with husband Frederic Stuenkle, two Missouri-born children, and her mother Mary/Dorothea Frerking on the Stuenkel farm.
immigration: Her widowed mother Dorothea emigrated to America with her children in 1845 aboard the ship General Washington from Bremen to New Orleans; Sophie Charlotte is documented in Missouri by the 1850 census.
records: 1850 U.S. census entry cited on WikiTree for Frederic and Charlotte Stinkle/Stuenkle in Lafayette County, Missouri.
Frerking family immigration record via the ship General Washington, cited on WikiTree.

bio: Johann Dietrich Frerking married Dorothea Maria Sprengel on 29 November 1811 in Esperke, Hanover. WikiTree records that he died on 15 July 1838 and was buried on 18 July 1838 in Esperke, before Dorothea and their children emigrated to Missouri.
records: WikiTree profile Frerking-57 for marriage, death, and burial notes.

bio: Dorothea Maria Sprengel was baptized in Esperke in April 1792 and married Johann Dietrich Frerking there in 1811. After Johann’s death, she emigrated with her children to America and was living in Lafayette County, Missouri by the 1850 census, in the household of daughter Sophie Charlotte and son-in-law Frederic Stuenkle. She died in 1868 and was buried in Saint Pauls Lutheran Cemetery in Concordia.
immigration: Dorothea emigrated in 1845 aboard the ship General Washington, departing Bremen and arriving at New Orleans on 24 November 1845.
records: FamilySearch passenger-list record QKNP-MJZR cited by WikiTree.
1850 U.S. census record MDZ8-FFP cited by WikiTree.
Find A Grave memorial #34625151 cited by WikiTree.

bio: WikiTree records Louis/Ludwig Scharnhorst as born in 1792 and dying in 1879 at Concordia, Lafayette County, Missouri. His burial is listed in Saint Pauls Lutheran Cemetery, tying the Scharnhorst family into the Concordia Lutheran cemetery network.
records: WikiTree profile Scharnhorst-89.
Find A Grave memorial #35135791 cited by WikiTree.

bio: Louise Gasdorff Scharnhorst is recorded on WikiTree as born in 1801 and dying on 17 October 1877. Her burial is listed in Saint Pauls Lutheran Cemetery, Concordia, Lafayette County, Missouri, alongside other German Lutheran families in this branch.
records: WikiTree profile Gasdorff-1.
Find A Grave memorial #35135768 cited by WikiTree.

bio: WikiTree’s Stuenkel surname page lists Johann Ludwig Stuenkel with a 5 March 1776 birth in Metel, Neustadt, Hanover, and an 18 April 1853 death in the Neustadt/Hannover area. The same surname page places him immediately among the Metel-to-Missouri Stuenkel profiles, including Fredrich Heinrich Konrad Stuenkel and Conrad Dietrich Friedrich Stuenkel.
records: WikiTree Stuenkel surname index entry for Johann Ludwig Stuenkel (profile Stuenkel-50).
